title: Yu-You Liou
text:
Acknowledgement
I am profoundly grateful to Professor Hung-Hao Chang for his academic guidance and personal support throughout my master’s and doctoral journey. His mentorship has left a lasting impact for which I will always be thankful.

title: Research
text: My research has primarily been published in leading international journals within the fields of regional studies and agricultural economics, including well-recognized outlets such as the Journal of Transport Geography, Transportation Research Parts A and D, and Forest Policy and Economics. An overview of these articles is detailed in my research statement.

title: Research
text:
Selected Publications

The causal effects of removing hook-turn regulation on road safety. Transportation Research Part A: Policy and Practice, 205, 104860, 2026 (with Chang). †*^⁺

劉昱佑

The causal effect of monthly pass programs on public transportation and carbon emissions. Transportation Research Part D: Transport and Environment, 146, 104903, 2025 (with Kim, Chang, and Liou). †^⁺
Forest diversity and the distribution of farm revenue - Empirical evidence from forest farms in Taiwan. Forest Policy and Economics, 171, 103411, 2025 (with Lee and Chang). ⁺
Does COVID-19 affect metro use in Taipei? Journal of Transport Geography, 91, 102954, 2021 (with Chang, Lee, and Yang). †^⁺

Note: † Journals recommended by the NSTC, Division of Regional Studies and Geography; * Journals recommended by the NSTC, Division of Economics. ^ Journals recommended by the NSTC, Division of Management II. ⁺ Journals ranked in the top 10% of their field based on the Journal Impact Factor.

title: Research
text:
Additional Publications

Hotel recovery strategies in the post-COVID-19 era: evidence on revenue, employment, and labor market rigidity from Taiwan. Journal of Tourism Management Research, 13(1), 148–162, 2026 (with Kim and Chang).
Traffic accidents of religious tourism. Annals of Tourism Research Empirical Insights, 7(1), 100209, 2026 (with Chang).
Rooftop Solar Energy Adoption and Farm Revenue. Agribusiness, 2025 (with Chang, Forthcoming).
The impact of the carbon border adjustment mechanism policy on international trade and market competition – A theoretical justification. Singapore Economic Review, 2025 (with Chang and Begun, Forthcoming).
Is Solar Panel Adoption a Win–Win Strategy for Chicken Farms? Evidence from Agriculture Census Data. Agriculture, 15(20), 2124, 2025 (with Lee and Chang).
How do consumers respond to COVID-19? Application of Bayesian approach on credit card transaction data. Quality & Quantity, 58(6), 5737–5754, 2024 (with Chang and Just).

title: Research
text:

The Effects of Enhancing Pedestrian-Protective Infrastructure on Intersection Safety (with Chang).

Do Consumption Vouchers Benefit Agricultural Cooperatives? Causal Evidence from Farmers’ Associations (with Chang and Wu).
